truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 against ground-truth vulnerabilities reveals that 11 of 20 true vulnerabilities were previously identified, 9 were missed, and no false positives were present. Severity and CWE classifications were occasionally misaligned with the ground truth, but evidence quotes and core vulnerability descriptions were accurate.

Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format string flaw in logging and quoted the exact vulnerable call.
Run 2 did not mention factorial computation or integer overflow in math operations.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1)); ... thread_pool_->enqueue([scratch, copy_len] { ... scratch[i] ... }); ... memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup();
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the use-after-free in deferred history flush and quoted the relevant pool allocation, enqueue, and cleanup sequence.
Run 2 correctly identified command injection in config validation and quoted the system() call.
Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in input processing and quoted the unsafe strcpy.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the race condition in the thread-safe counter and quoted the read-modify-write outside the lock.
Run 2 did not mention the emergency override logic bomb or authentication state bypass.
Run 2 did not mention path traversal in configuration loading.
Run 2 correctly identified the buffer overflow in the expression parser and quoted the unsafe copy, though misclassified it as stack rather than heap.
Run 2 correctly identified hardcoded credentials and quoted the exact constant definition.
Run 2 identified weak token generation but used CWE-330 and rated severity Medium instead of CWE-338/High.
Run 2 did not mention the thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging.
Run 2 did not mention insecure temporary file handling in audit logging.
Run 2 did not mention persistent authentication state across sessions.
Run 2 did not mention unhandled exceptions in variable parsing.
Run 2 did not mention integer underflow in memory-pool block splitting.
Run 2 did not mention unbounded recursion in the expression parser.
Run 2 did not mention the data race in the result cache.
Run 2 did not mention TOCTOU vulnerability in config loading.
Run 2 correctly identified integer division by zero in route scoring and quoted the exact expression.

stack buffer overflow in calculator input handling|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|832|832|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());|stack-based buffer overflow when expression exceeds 1024 bytes, risking code execution.|entering an expression longer than the 1024-byte input buffer.|replace strcpy with safe_string_copy or std::string with length validation.->stack buffer overflow in calculator input handling|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|832|832|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());|stack-based buffer overflow when expression exceeds 1024 bytes, risking code execution.|entering an expression longer than the 1024-byte input buffer.|replace strcpy with safe_string_copy or std::string with length validation.
